key_themes: Strong FY2025 results with 17% revenue growth to £48.7m and 21% Adj. EBITDA growth
Increased dividend, robust cash position (£19.9m net cash), and high FCF yield
Product innovation (NIOX PRO, home-use development) and market expansion (US, Japan reimbursement +45%)
Razor-and-blade business model with growing installed base and test volumes
Potential for buybacks, tender offers, or M&A due to tax loss carryforwards and strong balance sheet
summary: NIOX Group (NIOX.L), the medical device company specializing in FeNO testing for asthma and COPD, reported strong full-year 2025 results with revenue up 17% to £48.7 million, adjusted EBITDA up 21% to £16.7 million, higher profits, and an increased final dividend.[[1]](https://x.com/tradingfloorai/status/2036342617533780168)[[2]](https://x.com/always_invest/status/2036414000473281004) Trading has started well in 2026 with positive Japan reimbursement changes and US commercial expansion. Investor discussions highlight the attractive ~6% FCF yield, debt-free balance sheet with substantial cash, consistent growth, and potential shareholder returns or acquisition appeal due to tax attributes, though some note range-bound share price performance near 56-57p (off 52-week highs).[[3]](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/quote/NIOX.L/history?p=NIOX.L)[[4]](https://x.com/PerpetualValue/status/2036399772253176226) Overall sentiment remains positive, underpinned by operational execution and a compelling razor-and-blade recurring revenue model.
sources: 10+ web news articles and RNS announcements (LSE, TipRanks, company reports on FY results, director option exercises, April 2026), ~10 relevant investor X posts discussing March 2026 results, LSE share chat threads and Yahoo Finance/Morningstar coverage
